content.full_text:
Farn Porrrrcar- PnecrrcEs ConrMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-566O. Fax (916) 322-0886
Iuly 29,2003
John C. Elliott
Marsh Risk & Insurance Services
3 Embarcadero Center
San Francisco, CA 94lll
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-03-051
Dear Mr. Elliott:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Marsh Risk &
Insurance Services regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act,).I
We are updating you on the stafus of your request, as well as withdrawing your request at
this time.
Marsh USA, Inc. (d.b.a. Marsh Risk & Insurance Services, hereinafter "Marsh,)
entered into a contract with CaIPERS to provide insurance brokerage services for its real
estate portfolio. All services provided by Marsh are pursuant to the terms and conditions
of the Brokerage and Consulting Services Agreement, Contract200l-2694 (effective
May 1, 2002) entered into by and between calpERS and Marsh USA, Inc.
You have been advised by CaIPERS that certain individuals working for Marsh
are "consultants" and have therefore been asked to file a Form 700 in connection with
this contract. You have also been advised that these individuals are subject to the gift,
honoraria and travel provisions of the Act. As such, you are seeking advice as to whether
certain Marsh employees are, in fact, "consultants" and therefore subject to disclosure
and filing requirements in the calPF.RS conflict of interest code.
I Govern-"nt Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regula,tions.


File No. W-03-051
Page No. 2
As we discussed in an earlier conversation, we are governed by regulation
18329.5, copy enclosed, regarding conflict of interest code advice. Since the FppC is the
code reviewing body for CaIPERS, you must first request a determination from CalpERS
regarding the disclosure category and filing obligations for the Marsh employees for
whom you inquire. (Regulation18329.5 (a)Q).)
Please note that a request for written advice to the Commission does not alter the
filingobligations of any individual. (Sections 87300-87302(b);regulation 18730
(bX5XB).) Regulation 18730 (bX5XB) provides that an assuming office statement
should be filed within 30 days after assuming the designated position.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Legal Division
Enclosure

Ms. Laura Mattley, CaIPERS
Ms. Dixie Howard, FPPC

sections.question: whether certain Marsh employees are, in fact, "consultants" and therefore subject to disclosure and filing requirements in the CalPERS conflict of interest code
sections.conclusion: Since the FPPC is the code reviewing body for CalPERS, the requestor must first request a determination from CalPERS regarding the disclosure category and filing obligations for the Marsh employees in question. The FPPC is withdrawing the request at this time.
sections.question_synthetic: Marsh Risk & Insurance Services sought advice on whether certain Marsh employees working under a CalPERS contract are "consultants" subject to Form 700 filing and conflict of interest code disclosure requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice and instead directed the requestor to seek a determination from CalPERS first, as CalPERS is the agency responsible for making determinations under its conflict of interest code. Requesting advice from the FPPC does not alter any individual's filing obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter. The document contains a clear question and explanation of the FPPC's position, but the conclusion is that the FPPC is declining to provide substantive advice and referring the matter to CalPERS. Regulation 18329.5 is cited as the basis for this referral.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether certain Marsh employees are, in fact, "consultants" and therefore subject to disclosure and filing requirements in the CalPERS conflict of interest code

CONCLUSION: Since the FPPC is the code reviewing body for CalPERS, the requestor must first request a determination from CalPERS regarding the disclosure category and filing obligations for the Marsh employees in question. The FPPC is withdrawing the request at this time.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdraws Marsh Risk & Insurance Services' request for advice regarding whether certain Marsh employees are consultants subject to CalPERS conflict of interest code disclosure requirements, directing them to seek a determination from CalPERS first instead.
